answer: I'm Muhammad Ahmad Chughtai, a Full-Stack AI Application Engineer based in Lahore, Pakistan. I build AI-driven web applications — robust async backends, focused frontends, and the careful work in between. Currently finishing my BSCS at UMT while building personal projects on the side.

I've been building software for about 3 years on my own. Most of it is Django + React with a heavy AI bent: multimodal pipelines, RAG systems, and the async plumbing that keeps fast UIs snappy when the AI work is slow.

Full-stack by default. Python (Django + DRF, FastAPI) on the backend, React 18 + Vite + Tailwind on the frontend, and a heavy AI layer (RAG, ChromaDB, Gemini, OpenAI, Groq). PostgreSQL for data, Docker + Railway/Vercel for hosting, GSAP when something needs to move.

Python is my default backend weapon. Most of my recent work uses Django 5 with DRF, JWT auth, Celery for background work, and Pydantic-shaped validation. I care about request budgets — the AI calls happen off the request path so the user-facing endpoint stays under 500ms.

React 18 + Vite for production frontends, Tailwind + CSS variables for styling. I lean on async/await throughout, use React Query or SWR for server state, and keep components small enough that prop-drilling never gets out of hand.

I build RAG systems, async LLM pipelines, and tool-using agents. My current stack: Groq + Gemini + OpenAI in a fallback rotation, ChromaDB for vector storage, Gemini embeddings, and WeasyPrint for PDF generation. I handle rate limits and context formatting deliberately — the goal is outputs that don't make things up.

PostgreSQL for production, SQLite for local dev. I model relationships deliberately — TackTack alone has tables for daily-to-yearly recurrence, hierarchical subtasks, and per-user isolation. ORM is Django's, and I reach for raw SQL when the query plan matters.

Docker + Docker Compose for local parity, Railway + Vercel for hosting small services, Linux (Arch + Ubuntu) for day-to-day. I keep the multi-container topology honest — Django, frontend, ChromaDB, and a worker all run as separate services in compose.

Two personal projects: whiteboard (multimodal AI study app — Gemini turns lecture audio, photos, and PDFs into Markdown notes, with a RAG chat panel) and tacktack (AI task manager — natural-language scheduling via Groq, hierarchical subtasks, per-user data isolation). Both are deployed.

Best way to reach me: ahmadchughtai21@gmail.com. I'm open to internships, freelance and contract work. Comfortable integrating into teams or taking full ownership of a build. I usually reply within a day.
Lahore, Pakistan. Comfortable working remote-first with US and EU time zones; happy to overlap a few hours daily.
BSCS at the University of Management and Technology (UMT), Lahore — 2023 to present. Coursework spans Deep Learning, OS, Algorithms, Data Structures, and Databases.
I work in English day-to-day. I'm a native Urdu speaker too, happy to take meetings in either.
